title: Universal Response Envelope
description: Single source of truth for the Universal Response Envelope. All implementations (Go, TypeScript, PHP) MUST conform to this schema. See ADR.md for rationale.

Results.description: Payload — always an array, even for single items or empty responses.

Navigation.description: Pagination links. Present only for paginated list responses.

Errors.description: Error details. Present only when HasAnyErrors is true AND reporting is enabled.

MethodsStack.description: Debug call-chain trace. Present only when enabled in config.

IsFailed.description: true if the request failed (inverse of IsSuccess).

RequestedAt.description: The Go backend endpoint that handled the request.

RequestDelegatedAt.description: REQUIRED when the Go backend proxied/delegated the request to any downstream service. Must contain the full URL of the downstream endpoint (e.g., 'https://example.com/wp-json/...'). Empty string only when the request was NOT delegated.

SessionId.description: Session identifier for linking with session-level diagnostics.

HasAnyErrors.description: true if any errors occurred (even partial).

IsSingle.description: true if Results contains exactly one item.

IsMultiple.description: true if Results contains multiple items.

IsEmpty.description: true when Results is empty (TotalRecords is 0 or Results array has no items).

TotalRecords.description: Total number of records across all pages.

NextPage.description: URL for the next page, null if on last page.

PrevPage.description: URL for the previous page, null if on first page.

CloserLinks.description: Sliding window of nearby page URLs.

BackendMessage.description: Primary error message from the Go backend.

DelegatedServiceErrorStack.description: Stack trace lines from downstream PHP/other services. Legacy field — prefer DelegatedRequestServer for structured data.

Backend.description: Stack trace lines from the Go backend.

DelegatedRequestServer.description: REQUIRED when IsFailed=true AND RequestDelegatedAt is non-empty. Must contain structured error details from the delegated downstream server (PHP, Chrome extension, or any 3rd-party). Omitting this when a delegated request failed is a backend bug.

Backend.description: Ordered list of method calls in the Go backend.

LineNumber.description: Line number in the source file.

DelegatedRequestServer.description: Structured error details from a downstream delegated server (PHP/WordPress, Chrome extension, or any 3rd-party service). Populated when the Go backend proxied a request that failed.

DelegatedEndpoint.description: Exact URL of the downstream endpoint that was called.

Method.description: HTTP method used for the delegated request (GET, POST, etc.).

StatusCode.description: HTTP status code returned by the delegated server.

RequestBody.description: Request body sent to the delegated server. Null for GET requests.

Response.description: Full response body from the delegated server. Should include stacktrace if the delegated server supports structured error responses.

StackTrace.description: Stack trace lines from the delegated server (e.g., PHP backtrace, Node.js stack).

AdditionalMessages.description: Human-readable context or diagnostic message about the delegated error.
